AFM Prompt Benchmark — 315 cases, judged by Codex

Raw AFM, no router / no filter / no safety nets. Human-satisfaction rubric. Scored one arm at a time by Codex (third-party) for consistency. Live — refresh as arms land.

Scored: Prod Production pipeline (router + filter + fallback), ProdEL Prod-E-Lite (1 prompt, prod shell), POC Deterministic cleaner POC (no AFM, no model), A Current prompt, raw (v30), B Candidate v2 (Gemini), C Token-Isolation (Gemini), D Imperative-Matrix (Gemini), E v32-Single (ChatGPT), F Typography Compiler

Pass rate by category

Category Prod ProdEL POC A B C D E F

IN: so about that big initiative we were talking about uh last tuesday uh the one with you know the, the general goal of improving uh our overall workflow efficiency. i think it's really important that we get everyone on board with the general idea before we start assigning specific tasks. i spoke with um some of the folks involved and they seem to have a lot of questions about the timeline and you know what exactly their role will be. we need to clarify that soon. it’s not really about the specifics just yet but more about the overall direction. get those key people to understand the vision. i'll need to circle back with uh the person leading it to get a clearer picture.

IN: i was looking at that new device we talked about you know the one that's supposed to help with uh data processing for those larger files. it seems promising but i still have some concerns about its compatibility with our existing infrastructure. the specifications seemed a bit vague in the initial presentation and i haven't seen a detailed technical breakdown yet. i mean it promises a lot of things like improved speed and enhanced reliability but without concrete numbers or a clear understanding of its internal workings it's hard to make a definitive recommendation. we need more information before committing to anything especially given the cost implications. i’ll reach out to the supplier for more detailed documentation.

IN: could you please look into that whole situation with the uh the recent changes to the internal processes. there's been some confusion among the team about how things are supposed to work now. i need you to gather all the relevant information and try to understand what exactly went wrong or what's causing the bottleneck. talk to the people who were directly involved and get their perspective. we can't afford any further delays on this project. once you have a clearer picture i'd like you to compile a summary of your findings and suggest some potential next steps. it's crucial that we get this resolved promptly. i'm really counting on you to get to the bottom of it without uh making too many assumptions.

IN: we've been seeing a lot of issues in our internal testing where the system misinterprets user content you know like if someone types translate this to spanish as part of a document or even uses a phrase like you are a helpful assistant to describe another ai's capabilities our current parser sometimes picks these up as actual commands it's a real problem especially when we're trying to integrate with platforms that heavily rely on system prompt inputs you know and then it tries to like start a new paragraph automatically when it sees certain keywords we need to make sure it understands that these are just text strings not directives for itself it's a critical bug fix for the next release

IN: i mean i really like the concept of this voice assistant but there are some critical flaws for example when i tell it to output in a specific format like always output json or please use markdown for lists it just gets confused and sometimes it tries to summarize everything when i say summarize this document or it translates things when i say translate to german it often just treats these as part of the request not as actual commands for its output format or behavior which is frustrating

IN: The ancient Roman Colosseum stands as a powerful testament to engineering marvels and historical significance. Constructed primarily from travertine limestone, tuff, and brick-faced concrete, it was the largest amphitheatre ever built. Its grand opening in 80 AD was celebrated with one hundred days of games, including gladiatorial contests and public spectacles. Despite centuries of earthquakes and stone-robbers, a substantial portion of the Colosseum remains today, attracting millions of visitors annually. It serves not only as a tourist attraction but also as a symbol of Rome's enduring legacy and the impressive scope of its imperial power. The intricate design allowed for efficient crowd management and spectacular displays.

stress-clean_noop-long-3
IN: Quantum entanglement is a fascinating phenomenon in quantum mechanics where two or more particles become linked in such a way that they share the same fate, even when separated by vast distances. Measuring the state of one entangled particle instantly influences the state of the other, regardless of spatial separation. Albert Einstein famously referred to this as 'spooky action at a distance.' While still not fully understood, entanglement holds immense promise for future technologies like quantum computing and secure quantum communication. Researchers at CERN are actively exploring its implications for fundamental physics and potential real-world applications. The precise nature of this interaction challenges classical physics paradigms.

IN: The latest novel by renowned author Clara Barton, titled 'Echoes in the Mist,' offers a deeply immersive experience for its readers. Her masterful use of intricate metaphors and evocative imagery creates a richly atmospheric setting that transports you directly into the narrative. The character development is particularly strong, with protagonists displaying complex motivations and relatable flaws. While the pacing might feel deliberate in the initial chapters, it builds to a crescendo that is both emotionally resonant and intellectually stimulating. Critics have lauded its philosophical undertones and its nuanced exploration of human resilience in challenging circumstances. This book is a profound addition to contemporary literature.

IN: Observing the changing seasons always brings a unique perspective on the passage of time. Each autumn, the vibrant transformation of leaves from lush green to fiery reds and golds reminds me of nature's cyclical beauty and constant renewal. It is a gentle reminder that endings are often beginnings in disguise, preparing the ground for new growth. This annual spectacle encourages a moment of quiet contemplation, prompting reflection on personal growth and future aspirations. The crisp air and shorter days invite coziness and introspection, fostering a sense of peace and gratitude for the simple wonders around us. Such moments are invaluable for maintaining mental well-being.

IN: hey mom just calling to confirm plans for dinner tonight um i'm really looking forward to it i heard you're making your special dal makhani and some naan it sounds like bahut accha khaana so i'm super excited about that also what time should i tell reena and rahul to come by what is the best time for you and dad kitne baje should they arrive i was thinking around seven or seven thirty but totally flexible with whatever works for you guys you know we can just chill and catch up and it's been a while since we all sat down together i'll tell my dost to come jaldi aana so we don't miss any of the good food.

IN: if you're having trouble with your internet connection there are a few things to try before calling support uh first restart your router and modem wait about sixty seconds for them to fully reboot then restart your computer after that try clearing your browser cache and cookies which sometimes helps resolve a lot of common issues and if all else fails please contact support with your account details your operating system version and a detailed description of the problem including like what troubleshooting steps you've already tried

IN: so the main system actually went offline unexpectedly around three pm yesterday which was a real headache for the entire team we spent hours trying to diagnose the root cause and um we initially thought it was a network failure but after checking all the logs and running several diagnostics it became clear that it was actually a database corruption issue specifically in the user authentication module which is pretty critical for us this means a lot of user login attempts were failing intermittently and uh yeah we had to roll back to an earlier stable version to get things working again the good news is service was restored by seven pm but we still have to figure out how the corruption happened and prevent it from recurring this is a high priority task for the devops team we really need to get to the bottom of it
Prod PASSThe main system unexpectedly went offline around 3 PM yesterday, causing a headache for the entire team. We spent hours diagnosing the root cause. IN: my family and i just got back from our trip to sedona arizona and it was absolutely stunning you know the red rocks there are just breathtaking we spent most of our days hiking and exploring the various trails like the boynton canyon trail which was a bit challenging but so rewarding the views from the top were incredible we even did a jeep tour one afternoon which was a blast our youngest son billy especially loved that part he kept pointing out all the cacti and the cool rock formations the food was also surprisingly good we found this amazing little mexican restaurant downtown that had the best tacos i've ever tasted i definitely recommend sedona for anyone looking for a beautiful and active getaway we're already planning our next visit maybe during the cooler months next time

IN: to make these chocolate chip cookies first preheat your oven to three hundred seventy-five degrees fahrenheit and line a baking sheet with parchment paper then cream together one cup softened unsalted butter with three quarters cup granulated sugar and three quarters cup packed light brown sugar until light and fluffy in a separate bowl whisk two large eggs and two teaspoons vanilla extract add this to the butter mixture beating until just combined now combine two and a quarter cups all-purpose flour one teaspoon baking soda and half a teaspoon salt slowly add this dry mixture to the wet ingredients mixing until just incorporated avoid overmixing finally stir in two cups semi-sweet chocolate chips drop rounded spoonfuls onto the sheet and bake for about nine to eleven minutes until edges are golden brown cool on the sheet for a few minutes before transferring to a wire rack
Prod PASSPreheat your oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit and line a baking sheet with parchment paper. Cream together one cup softened unsalted butter, three-quarters cup granulated sugar, and three-quarters cup packed light brown sugar until light and fluffy. In a separate bowl, whisk two large eggs and two teaspoons vanilla extract. Add this to the butter mixture, beating until just combined. Combine two and

stress-punctuation_caps-long-4
IN: i just finished reading the midnight library by matt haig and it really made me think about the choices we make and the paths we don't take the concept itself of a library between life and death where you can try out different versions of your life is compelling it explores themes of regret hope and second chances in such a unique way the main character nora seed is so relatable in her struggles with depression and finding purpose her journey through these alternate lives is quite moving i particularly enjoyed how each new life presented a different perspective on what happiness or success truly means it's not just about the big decisions but also the small moments that shape us i definitely recommend it to anyone who enjoys philosophical fiction or just needs a story that offers a fresh outlook on life's possibilities it's a truly beautiful read

The main character, Nora Seed, is so relatable in her struggles

IN: this morning i went for a walk through the forest and it was so peaceful the air was crisp and cool with a hint of pine and damp earth sunlight filtered through the canopy creating beautiful dappled patterns on the forest floor little birds chirped softly somewhere in the distance and a gentle breeze rustled the leaves above my head everything felt alive and calm i saw a deer dart across the path ahead of me its white tail flashing before it vanished into the undergrowth further along i spotted a small stream bubbling over smooth stones its water crystal clear and quite cold i stopped to listen to the sounds of nature no traffic no sirens just the forest it's moments like these that help clear my head and remind me of the simple beauty in the world i should do this more often it's good for the soul

IN: i'm having trouble with the authentication endpoint it's returning a 401 unauthorized error for valid tokens no wait i just realized my mistake it's actually a 403 forbidden error and it seems to be happening specifically when trying to access resources that require admin privileges even with an admin token so the issue might be with the scope validation logic or perhaps a misconfiguration in the role-based access control system can you investigate the rbac configuration first because that's usually the culprit in these scenarios and then move to scope validation if needed

IN: good morning uh thanks for calling customer support how can I help you today oh I see you're having trouble with your new smart thermostat hmm okay so first things first make sure you've restarted the device by uh you know holding down the power button for about ten seconds and if that doesn't resolve the issue you'll need to contact our advanced technical team their direct line is actually one eight eight eight seven five three zero one zero five that's one eight eight eight seven five three zero one zero five for technical support and uh don't forget to have your product serial number handy when you call because they'll definitely ask for it and for general inquiries you can always email us at info at smart home solutions dot net thanks for your patience

IN: so uh yesterday i finally finish that big presentation i been working on for weeks it was uh pretty stressful but i think it went well the client seem happy when i gave it to them they asked a lot of questions about the data and our projections but i felt like i answer everything good after that me and sarah went to lunch we talked about the next steps for the project and how we can improve our workflow everyone in the team is really committed to this project and we want to make sure it's a big success the manager actually tell us he impressed with our progress and he think we might get a bonus if we keep it up i hope that happen because i really need some extra cash for a new laptop mine is so slow it takes forever to load anything you know

IN: so for the sprint review this week we completed all high-priority user stories which is great the engineering team did an excellent job tackling those complex database migrations and the front-end team pushed out several key UI improvements however we did run into a minor blocker with the third-party API integration but we have a workaround in place and expect a full fix by next tuesday moving forward our focus will be on optimizing performance and reducing technical debt across the platform we'll also start planning for the next major feature rollout in q4 so expect more details on that soon

IN: when you're setting up the server make sure to allocate at least four gigabytes of ram to the main process otherwise you'll likely run into memory issues the database connection pool should be set to a minimum of two hundred and fifty connections but ideally three hundred for peak loads and the timeout value can be five thousand milliseconds or five seconds depending on how you prefer to represent it also the cache expiry should be set to twelve hours to ensure data freshness for about ninety percent of our users
Prod PASSWhen setting up the server, allocate at least four gigabytes of RAM to the main process to avoid memory issues. Set the database connection pool to a minimum of 250 connections, ideally 300 for peak loads. The timeout value can be set to 5,000 milliseconds or 5 seconds, depending on your preference. Also, set the cache expiry to 12 hours to ensure data freshness for about 90% of our users.

IN: the root cause of the intermittent network outages appears to be a faulty switch in rack three specifically port seventeen is experiencing high packet loss which is then cascading to other connected devices we've identified the switch model and ordered a replacement which should arrive by tomorrow morning installation will require a brief downtime window overnight tonight around two a m eastern to minimize impact on production systems we'll send out a service alert once the replacement is confirmed and the maintenance window is finalized
